headline: Empirical lmhead12k (general 12,288 cut) FAILS / is at serious risk on the greedy-identity gate when served on the int4 base. PPL gate passes; TPS matches the int4 base. Root cause: kept_ids was selected from the BF16 model's argmax, but the served model is INT4, whose argmax shifts ~1.33% of decode steps to tokens the BF16 model never emits.

GATE__served_pruned_vs_plain_eager_prune.reference: reference_offline_pruned.jsonl (vLLM offline enforce_eager greedy, SAME pruned ckpt)
GATE__served_pruned_vs_plain_eager_prune.candidate: decode_outputs.jsonl (vLLM served pruned ckpt via harness)
GATE__served_pruned_vs_plain_eager_prune.caveat: Divergence is numerical (median first-div ~token 98, prompts align byte-for-byte). It reflects vLLM served path (cudagraph + continuous/chunked-prefill batching) vs offline enforce_eager numerics, AMPLIFIED by pruning-induced near-ties (clipping the true top logit leaves near-ties that flip under tiny perturbations). Whether THE official gate fails depends on the organizers' exact reference-generation config, which is approximated here by vLLM offline enforce_eager.

PRUNE_EFFECT__int4full_vs_int4pruned.note: Clean apples-to-apples: identical engine/config, only the checkpoint (full vs pruned lm_head) differs. Isolates the PRUNING effect, config-independent.

WRONG_REFERENCE__served_pruned_vs_bf16_s.note: NOT the gate. int4 quantization legitimately diverges from bf16 (an allowed numeric optimization per program.md). Kept only to show this is ~total divergence and must not be used as the reference.

bf16_stock_per_token_out_of_kept.why: kept_ids hard-includes every bf16 decode emission BY CONSTRUCTION

root_cause.interpretation: Quantization (bf16 -> int4 W4A16) shifts ~1.33% of greedy argmax decisions to tokens the bf16 model never emitted. Because kept_ids was selected to cover the BF16 model's argmax, it does NOT cover those int4-specific argmax tokens; pruning clips them, and each clip cascades.

fix_direction_counterfactual.effect: covers THIS public int4 decode to 0% out-of-kept
fix_direction_counterfactual.remaining_risk: still public-tailored; the PRIVATE int4 argmax distribution remains uncovered (the 'not adversarially safe' caveat). A general int4-self-consistent kept set + config-matched gate re-run is required before any submission.

verdict: NEGATIVE / INVALID-AS-BUILT for the leaderboard greedy-identity gate. The lm_head bandwidth lever (21.3x general / 29.75x tight) and PPL safety (1.9767) are real, but selecting kept_ids from the BF16 model while serving INT4 breaks greedy identity on the public prompts. TPS is neutral vs the int4 base at single-stream (expected; lm_head GEMM is a small fraction without a drafter).
